Note that some subtleties or contextual meanings may not be perfectly captured in a direct translation, as is often the case with language translations.

अगर आप खुश हो ना तो मुझे लगता है उनकी आधे - Agar aap khush ho na to mujhe lagta hai unki aadhe

The provided text appears to convey that the emotional state of a person, presumably a parent (mother or father), can affect their children. The idea presented is that happiness or unhappiness can be transferred to or felt by others, particularly by one's children. The last sentence seems to express that if you (the parent) are happy, then the speaker believes that half of the children's... The sentence is incomplete and ends abruptly, indicating that the message is only partially provided.

दुख चले जाते हैं। औलाद का सुख,
औलाद का दुख तभी तो बोला हुआ।

Combining with the previous segment to give context, the Hindi sentences you provided, written in Roman script (transliteration), are as follows:

वह उनको उनको लग जाता है। वह मां बाप है। तो - Woh unko unko lag jaata hai. Woh maan baap hai. To
अगर आप खुश हो ना तो मुझे लगता है उनकी आधे - Agar aap khush ho na to mujhe lagta hai unki aadhe
दुख चले जाते हैं। औलाद का सुख, - Dukh chale jaate hain. Aulaad ka sukh,
औलाद का दुख तभी तो बोला हुआ। - Aulaad ka dukh tabhi to bola hua.

In English, these sentences suggest that the emotional state of the parents can be transmitted to their children, and if the parents are happy, it is believed that much of the children's sorrow disappears. It touches on the deep connection between parents' well-being and their children's happiness, referencing a saying about the joy and sorrow of children. It could be roughly translated into English as:

"It affects them greatly, and that's for parents. So if you are happy, then I think half of their sorrows go away. The happiness of the children, the sorrow of the children, it's only then mentioned."

The overall message implies how crucial the emotional stability of parents is in influencing the emotional well-being of their children.
